title: US forces enter Iran by..?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify, however intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: US forces enter Iran by April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify; however, intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Note: Only US military personnel who deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran for operational purposes (e.g., military, humanitarian, etc.) will qualify. Pilots who are shot down, or other cases in which US military personnel do not deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran, will not qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Israel aerial strikes on Iranian military targets, ongoing since February 28, have escalated into ground operations with US special forces conducting rescues of downed F-15 pilots deep inside Iran on April 5, boosting trader consensus for entry by April 30 amid $236 million in volume. Pentagon preparations signal weeks of limited ground raids, potentially targeting Kharg Island oil facilities or coastal sites, supported by over 50,000 US troops deployed regionally—the largest buildup since 2003. President Trump's ultimatums demand reopening the Strait of Hormuz or face infrastructure attacks on bridges and power plants, with recent pauses extended to April 6 amid stalled ceasefire talks, heightening risks of rapid escalation or de-escalation via diplomacy.

title: US x Iran ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another, or for an official ceasefire agreement to be otherwise confirmed to have been reached by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Israeli airstrikes targeted Iranian oil facilities on Kharg Island, rail infrastructure, and buildings in northern Iran as recently as April 7, intensifying the conflict that erupted February 28 with attacks on Tehran's leadership, nuclear sites, and military assets. President Trump issued an April 8 deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz—blocked since late February, spiking global oil prices—or face raids on power plants and bridges, warning of catastrophic consequences. Iran rejected US ceasefire proposals and a 45-day mediated plan from Pakistan, issuing counter-demands while denying direct negotiations. Diplomatic efforts by regional mediators continue amid mutual escalation signals, with trader consensus reflecting low near-term de-escalation prospects but potential for talks post-deadline.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain leads trader consensus at 16% implied probability after a flawless European qualifying campaign topped by their Euro 2024 triumph and strong March friendlies, including a 3-0 win over Serbia, showcasing midfield depth with Rodri managed carefully. France follows closely at 13.5% buoyed by a recent 2-1 friendly victory over Brazil highlighting Mbappé's form, while England's 11.3% reflects steady group-topping qualification amid Southgate's continuity. Argentina (9.2%) and Brazil (8.6%) round out the bunched top five via CONMEBOL dominance—Argentina first, Brazil fifth—but the expanded 48-team format advancing 32 from 12 groups fosters parity, with no dominant favorite emerging post-qualifiers' dramatic playoffs last week securing spots for Sweden, Türkiye, Bosnia-Herzegovina, and Czechia.

title: Real Madrid CF vs. FC Bayern München
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Tuesday, April 7, 2026 between Real Madrid CF and FC Bayern München.

markets.question: Will Real Madrid CF win on 2026-04-07?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 7, 2026
If Real Madrid CF wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: FC Bayern München's clinical strikes from Luis Díaz in the 41st minute and Harry Kane seconds into the second half propelled them to a 2-1 UEFA Champions League quarterfinal first-leg victory at the Bernabéu, cementing trader consensus at 100% for a Bayern win as Real Madrid's late Kylian Mbappé goal proved insufficient against Manuel Neuer's nine saves and resolute defending. Bayern's superior shot conversion and even possession (52%) overcame Real Madrid's home advantage and second-half pressure, with no red cards disrupting flow. While official UEFA confirmation resolves the market, rare scenarios like successful protest or scoreline amendment could theoretically challenge the outcome, though Bayern's lead sets them up strongly for the second leg in Munich.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices California Governor Gavin Newsom as the frontrunner at 26% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, in a fragmented field lacking an incumbent after the party's 2024 defeat, buoyed by his recent book tour, national media appearances teasing a run, and consistent leads in early national polls from February-March 2026. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follows at 9%, drawing progressive enthusiasm through viral advocacy but facing electability concerns outside urban bases; Jon Ossoff's 6% reflects Georgia's battleground appeal as a young senator. Differentiation hinges on executive experience (Newsom, governors like Shapiro) versus ideological energy (AOC) or swing-state wins (Ossoff, Kelly); 2026 midterms, fundraising tallies, and DNC endorsements could consolidate momentum ahead of primaries.

title: The Masters - Winner
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler's two green jackets from 2022 and 2024, plus an unblemished top-20 Augusta National record, anchor his trader consensus at 13.5% implied probability despite withdrawing from last week's Houston Open for his second child's birth and recent subpar finishes. The tightly bunched top odds reflect a wide-open field, with defending champion Rory McIlroy's 6.5% tempered by a prior back withdrawal and repeat-winner rarity, while Jon Rahm (7.8%) and Bryson DeChambeau (6.5%) gain traction from LIV momentum and strong recent major contention. Collin Morikawa's back spasms add uncertainty, amplifying Augusta's unpredictable course history dynamics ahead of Thursday's start.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim edge as trader consensus frontrunner for the 2028 Republican nomination, buoyed by his incumbency advantage, a recent CPAC straw poll naming him top GOP contender, and high-profile appearances like headlining the Rockbridge Network donor summit last week plus announcing a new book amid speculation. California Governor Gavin Newsom trails closely as the leading Democratic option, propelled by polls showing him trouncing Kamala Harris in a California primary matchup and steady positioning against Trump administration policies. Secretary of State Marco Rubio's odds have risen on foreign policy visibility during Iran tensions, narrowing the GOP field. With 2026 midterms looming as a key test of party strength, endorsements from President Trump, and potential scandals, the race remains tightly contested among early favorites, reflecting high uncertainty this far out.

title: Republican Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket places Robert F. Kennedy Jr. ahead at 49% for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination, driven by his prominent HHS secretary role implementing MAHA reforms like fraud crackdowns and prevention investments announced in February 2026, boosting his anti-establishment appeal despite low polling at 0-3%. J.D. Vance trails at 38% amid reports of donor preference for Marco Rubio—stemming from President Trump's Mar-a-Lago polling where most favored Rubio—and Vance's public indecision on a bid during the Iran conflict escalation. Rubio's odds at 21.6% reflect GOP donor shadow drafts and his foreign policy visibility as secretary of state, highlighting a contested open primary post-Trump's term limit with 2026 midterms looming.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil futures have surged to around $115 per barrel amid escalating US-Iran tensions and Middle East uncertainty, marking a 4-week high and 22% gain over the past month, as supply disruption fears outweigh building US inventories, which rose 5.5 million barrels in the week ended March 27 per latest EIA data. OPEC+ agreed on April 5 to modestly raise May output quotas by 206,000 barrels per day—largely symbolic given production constraints in conflict zones—reflecting trader consensus on persistent geopolitical premiums despite softening global demand signals. Key watchpoints include weekly EIA inventory releases every Wednesday, potential Strait of Hormuz developments, and the IEA Oil Market Report later this month, which could recalibrate supply-demand balances through April's end.

title: Sporting CP vs. Arsenal FC
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Tuesday, April 7, 2026 between Sporting CP and Arsenal FC.

markets.question: Will Sporting CP win on 2026-04-07?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 7, 2026
If Sporting CP wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's gritty 1-0 away win over Sporting CP in the UEFA Champions League quarterfinal first leg, clinched by Kai Havertz's 90+1' header from Gabriel Martinelli's cross, has cemented trader consensus at 100% implied probability for the Gunners' victory in this moneyline market. Despite absences like Bukayo Saka and Jurrien Timber, Mikel Arteta's tactical subs and David Raya's crucial saves preserved a club-record-equaling clean sheet against Viktor Gyökeres' blunt home attack, extending Arsenal's unbeaten head-to-head streak (D4 L0, including last season's 5-1 rout). Sporting's 16 straight home wins were halted, but the tie remains alive for the second leg at Emirates. Minimal challenges persist barring official UEFA scoreline reversal or protest, both remote possibilities.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' dominant W17 car has propelled teammates George Russell and Kimi Antonelli to the top of the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship standings after four Grands Prix, with Antonelli leading 72-63 points following back-to-back victories in China—where he ignored team orders—and Japan. Russell's recent Suzuka podium was marred by battery failure and untimely Safety Car deployment, yet trader consensus implies a 43.5% probability for the experienced Briton to prevail in their intra-team title duel, per Toto Wolff's directive allowing fair racing without contact. Antonelli's 30.7% reflects his rookie surge as the youngest-ever points leader, but experience edges Russell ahead. Ferrari's Charles Leclerc (49 points) and Lewis Hamilton trail distant third and fourth, underscoring Mercedes' 45-point Constructors' lead and car superiority under new regs.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's league-best 62-16 record and status as reigning NBA champions from 2025 fuel their 39.5% implied probability as trader consensus favorite, bolstered by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP frontrunner campaign and Chet Holmgren's impact in a 146-111 rout of Utah on April 5. San Antonio Spurs trail closely at 60-19 and 15.2%, riding Victor Wembanyama's postseason potential and Stephon Castle's triple-double in a 115-102 win over Philadelphia on April 6, positioning them as the West's top challenger. Boston Celtics' No. 2 East seed at 53-25 (11.8%) and Detroit Pistons' conference-leading 57-22 (5.5%) reflect solid paths, but West depth and OKC's home-court edge through playoffs starting April 18 shape the crowded field.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Israel airstrikes have intensified against Iran's oil facilities, rail networks, bridges, and power plants as President Trump's April 7 deadline looms for Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face further escalation, including threats to "destroy" civilian infrastructure. Iran rejected a Pakistani-brokered ceasefire on April 6, countering with demands for a permanent regional halt to hostilities, sanctions relief, and reconstruction aid amid ongoing missile exchanges with Israel and proxy attacks via Hezbollah and Houthis. US and Israeli forces report degrading two-thirds of Iran's missile production and nuclear sites since February 28, yet Tehran sustains limited retaliation, fueling trader uncertainty over de-escalation timelines despite diplomatic pressures from Qatar and Pakistan.

title: Real Madrid CF vs. FC Bayern München - More Markets
description: More markets for the UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET.

markets.question: Spread: FC Bayern München (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Bayern München" if FC Bayern München win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Real Madrid CF".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Real Madrid and Bayern Munich head into their UEFA Champions League quarter-final first leg on April 7 at the Bernabéu amid injury crises tilting trader sentiment toward a competitive tie. Madrid's Thibaut Courtois is ruled out for both legs with a thigh injury, forcing Andriy Lunin into goal, while Rodrygo remains sidelined long-term by an ACL tear and Éder Militão nears return from a muscle issue around April 1. Bayern faces a goalkeeper nightmare with Manuel Neuer (calf), Sven Ulreich (adductor), and Jonas Urbig (concussion) unavailable, plus Jamal Musiala out with a fibula injury. Madrid's home advantage and recent La Liga momentum counter Bayern's strong aggregate win over Atalanta, but vulnerabilities on both benches heighten upset potential.

title: Colombia Chamber of Representatives Election: 3rd Place
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Colombia on 8 March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Final tallies from Colombia's March 8, 2026, parliamentary elections, certified by the Registraduría Nacional after completed escrutinios, position the Partido Liberal Colombiano (PLC) with 26 seats in the 183-seat Chamber of Representatives, securing third place behind the Historic Pact for Colombia (PH) at 42 seats and Centro Democrático (CD) at 30. This outcome, driven by strong regional performances in proportional territorial circunscripciones via the D'Hondt method, underpins trader consensus implying 96% probability for PLC, reflecting the wisdom of crowds in a fragmented Congress where no party holds a majority. While special seats for peace victims and indigenous groups are allocated, realistic challenges to PLC's standing would require successful legal recounts or disputes in key departments, though none have gained traction in the past month.

title: Kansas City Royals vs. Cleveland Guardians
description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Kansas City Royals and Cleveland Guardians, scheduled for April 7 at 6:10PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Kansas City Royals" if the Kansas City Royals win the game.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cleveland Guardians host the Kansas City Royals in a pivotal early AL Central series at Progressive Field, with the home team holding a slight edge at 6-5 to the Royals' 5-5 amid cold weather conditions favoring low-scoring affairs. Guardians shortstop Gabriel Arias was placed on the 10-day IL with a left hamstring strain after exiting Monday's game, leading to second baseman Juan Brito's recall and potentially weakening infield defense. Probable starters Noah Cameron (1-0, 3.60 ERA for Royals) face Gavin Williams (Guardians), whose strong fastball could exploit Kansas City's .240 batting average against righties. Royals closer Carlos Estévez remains sidelined with a foot contusion, straining their late-inning options, while both clubs boast potent cores like Bobby Witt Jr. and José Ramírez driving trader focus on matchup edges and bullpen depth.

title: Oilers vs. Utah
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 7 at 9:30PM ET:
If the Oilers win, the market will resolve to "Oilers".
If the Utah win, the market will resolve to "Utah".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Edmonton Oilers face the Utah Mammoth at Delta Center with trader consensus shaped by severe injury setbacks to the Oilers' top line, as Leon Draisaitl remains sidelined with a lower-body issue—yet to skate and questionable even for playoff openers—and Zach Hyman out indefinitely with an undisclosed injury that could sideline him up to two weeks. These absences leave Edmonton shorthanded after a 5-1 loss to Vegas snapped their five-game win streak, testing depth against a Utah squad bolstered by Mackenzie Weegar's projected return from upper-body woes. Both clubs jockey for Pacific Division positioning—Oilers atop at 39-29-9—with Utah's home-ice edge, rest advantage, and historical struggles versus Edmonton factoring into a tightly contested matchup amid late-season playoff implications.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket crowns Finland as the clear frontrunner at 38.3% implied probability for Eurovision 2026 victory, propelled by Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen's commanding UMK national selection win on February 28 with "Liekinheitin"—a violin-pop fusion blending classical flair and high-energy hooks that has dominated early fan polls, OGAE votes (including France's 12 points), and betting markets. France (10.9%) trails closely via internal selection of 17-year-old Monroe's jury-favored "Regarde," while Denmark (10.3%) benefits from strong national final momentum. With all 35 songs unveiled, semi-final running orders recently revealed for Vienna's Wiener Stadthalle shows on May 12/14 and final May 16, traders eye televote-jury splits and stage impact as key swing factors in this unpredictable contest.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 97.5% implied probability to no change in the federal funds rate at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting, reflecting strong consensus driven by March's nonfarm payrolls surging 178,000—well above the 57,000 median forecast—while unemployment edged down to 4.3%, underscoring labor market resilience amid moderating inflation pressures. The March FOMC held rates steady at 3.50%-3.75%, with the dot plot projecting a median 3.4% by year-end 2026 via later cuts, not imminent action. This skin-in-the-game sentiment prices in balanced risks, though hotter-than-expected March CPI data due April 9 or dovish FOMC minutes tomorrow could marginally challenge the hold if revealing policy shifts.

This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final Low price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT Low prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

title: Thunder vs. Lakers
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 7 at 10:30PM ET:
If the Thunder win, the market will resolve to "Thunder".
If the Lakers win, the market will resolve to "Lakers".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Oklahoma City Thunder enter their road matchup against the Los Angeles Lakers as the Western Conference leaders with a 62-16 record, fresh off a dominant 139-96 blowout win over LA just five days ago where Shai Gilgeous-Alexander led the charge. Lakers trader sentiment reflects severe injury woes, with LeBron James ruled out for left foot management, Luka Dončić sidelined by a hamstring strain for the season's stretch run, Austin Reaves out with an oblique injury, and Marcus Smart unavailable due to an ankle issue—leaving a makeshift lineup featuring Luke Kennard at point guard alongside Rui Hachimura, Jarred Vanderbilt, and Deandre Ayton. OKC boasts a clean injury report and elite recent form, positioning them strongly for playoff seeding while the Lakers fight to hold third place amid back-to-back challenges and load management.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects the Iranian regime's demonstrated resilience following Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's assassination in late February 2026 U.S.-Israeli airstrikes, which sparked an ongoing war but failed to trigger mass uprisings or opposition breakthroughs. Despite economic strain, currency devaluation, and scattered protests, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has maintained control through brutal crackdowns—including April 2 executions of dissidents—while a successor leadership stabilized succession without factional fractures. No unified internal opposition or ground game has emerged in the past month, per analysts, rallying hardliners amid external threats. With three weeks until resolution, dramatic shifts like IRGC defections, total economic meltdown, or ground invasion would be needed to alter this trajectory.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: U.S. forces conducted airstrikes on military targets at Iran's Kharg Island early April 7, 2026, sparing critical oil infrastructure that handles over 90% of the country's crude exports through the Strait of Hormuz, according to U.S. officials and Iranian assessments confirming most facilities intact. President Trump set an 8 p.m. ET deadline for Iran to reopen the strait amid escalating conflict, with prior March strikes and Pentagon discussions of a potential ground seizure highlighting the island's strategic value. Iran has reinforced air defenses and personnel there recently. No verified loss of control has occurred, but traders monitor post-deadline diplomatic talks, further military actions, or retaliation risks that could tip the balance.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 8?
description:

title: Baltimore Orioles vs. Chicago White Sox
description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Baltimore Orioles and Chicago White Sox, scheduled for April 7 at 7:40PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Baltimore Orioles" if the Baltimore Orioles win the game.

This market will resolve to "Chicago White Sox" if the Chicago White Sox win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

This market will resolve to "No Run" if no runs are scored in the 1st inning by either team.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

To know when a postponed game will be played, please check the home team's schedule on MLB.com for the listed team and look for the game described as a makeup game.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trevor Rogers (2-0, 1.38 ERA) gives the Baltimore Orioles a strong edge on the mound against Chicago White Sox right-hander Shane Smith (0-2) in this AL matchup at Guaranteed Rate Field, where cold early April weather could suppress offense with an over/under around 6.5-7 runs. Both teams sit around 4-6 early in the season—Orioles third in the AL East, White Sox struggling in the AL Central—following a series opener won by Baltimore. Key absences include Orioles infielder Jackson Holliday (10-day IL, hamate) and starter Zach Eflin (60-day IL, elbow), plus White Sox outfielder Everson Pereira (ankle); recent head-to-head dominance favors the visitors, but Chicago's home bullpen depth offers upset potential amid tight trader consensus.

title: UEFA Champions League Winner
description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League (soccer).

markets.question: Will Real Madrid win the 2025–26 Champions League?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed team will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bayern Munich and Arsenal lead trader consensus at 29.5% and 26.5% implied probabilities after securing vital away wins in Champions League quarterfinal first legs—Bayern's 2-1 victory at Real Madrid and Arsenal's 1-0 at Sporting CP—extending their dominant round-of-16 aggregates of 10-2 over Atalanta and 3-1 versus Bayer Leverkusen. Topping the league phase with 21 and 24 points respectively, both boast strong form, key player availability, and favorable second-leg home advantages, keeping the race tight amid an open bracket. Barcelona (16.5%) and PSG (14.5%) lag with riskier ties against Atletico Madrid and Liverpool, while Real Madrid's home defeat underscores their struggles despite advancing past Manchester City.

title: Sporting CP vs. Arsenal FC - More Markets
description: More markets for the UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET.

markets.question: Spread: Sporting CP (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 7 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Sporting CP" if Sporting CP win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Arsenal FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sporting CP face Arsenal in the UEFA Champions League quarter-final first leg on April 7 at Estádio José Alvalade, with the hosts severely hampered by key absences: winger Nuno Santos sidelined by a thigh injury for around three weeks and captain Morten Hjulmand suspended, eroding defensive width and midfield control against Arsenal's high-pressing style. Both could return for the April 15 second leg at Emirates Stadium. Arsenal navigate their own injury concerns post-Carabao Cup final loss to Manchester City, with Mikel Merino out (foot), Eberechi Eze doubtful (month sidelined), and Martin Ødegaard, Jurrien Timber, Leandro Trossard as question marks, though no fresh issues reported recently. Arsenal boast nine UCL wins from ten this season, while Sporting reach their first quarter-final amid strong Primeira Liga form but recent fixture rescheduling for extra preparation. Home advantage and aggregate scoring favor a cautious Sporting approach, but trader consensus likely reflects Arsenal's edge in squad depth and momentum.

title: Colombia Presidential Election
description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a razor-thin first-round plurality lead for Candidate M at 49.5% in Colombia's May 31 presidential election, just ahead of leftist Historic Pact nominee Iván Cepeda Castro (43.5%) and Centro Democrático's Paloma Valencia (40.6%), reflecting post-primary fragmentation after March 9 congressional elections delivered President Petro's coalition the largest Senate bloc but no majority. Valencia's strong primary showing boosted her momentum on security and anti-corruption appeals, while Cepeda consolidated left support despite Petro's sagging approval; Abelardo de la Espriella's 13.5% splits the right. Recent polls like AtlasIntel favor Cepeda, highlighting market divergence toward moderates amid economic pressures. Debates, endorsements, or scandals could forge separation before the runoff if no candidate exceeds 50%.

title: Hornets vs. Celtics
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 7 at 8:00PM ET:
If the Hornets win, the market will resolve to "Hornets".
If the Celtics win, the market will resolve to "Celtics".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Boston Celtics host the Charlotte Hornets at TD Garden in a late-season matchup with playoff seeding implications, where trader consensus favors the home team amid a clean Celtics injury report signaling full health for stars like Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown. The Hornets face depth issues, with center PJ Hall out (ankle) and guard Coby White probable (groin soreness) per latest updates, potentially straining their backcourt and frontcourt rotation. Boston boasts a dominant head-to-head edge, winning eight of the last 10 regular-season meetings including a recent 118-89 Hornets upset on March 4, but holds superior recent form at 46-32 ATS overall and strong home splits. No rest disadvantages for either side, though Boston's home-court advantage and defensive efficiency could prove decisive.

title: Trump out as President by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump resigns or is removed as President or otherwise ceases to be the President of the United States for any period of time by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Donald Trump's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify. Temporary removal (e.g. temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment under Section 3 or a Section 4 invocation not sustained by both Houses of Congress) or impeachment without removal will not count.

A sustained invocation of the Twenty-Fifth Amendment, Section 4 (i.e., if both Houses of Congress, by two-thirds vote, uphold the Vice President and Cabinet’s determination of presidential inability) will qualify for a "Yes" resolution. 

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trump's ongoing military posturing against Iran, including a provocative April 1 national address warning of catastrophic retaliation and a successful U.S. pilot rescue highlighted in his April 6 press conference, has fueled Democratic calls—such as from Reps. Tlaib, Quigley, and Jeffries—for Cabinet invocation of the 25th Amendment, alongside rare criticism from Sen. Murkowski and fringe voices like MTG urging de-escalation. Despite this partisan noise, trader consensus at 97.8% "No" stems from insurmountable barriers: Republican House and Senate majorities block impeachment and conviction, a loyal Cabinet precludes Section 4 action, and no resignation or health indicators exist early in Trump's second term. Late-breaking escalation, scandal, or medical emergency could theoretically shift odds before April 30 resolution.
